title: Deduplicate equivalent work and isolate validation state
goal: Prevent redundant Grabowski tasks and full validations while eliminating interference from shared temporary validation state, without reusing stale evidence or skipping checks required by a changed head, environment or risk profile.

acceptance.assertion: Durable work obtains a deterministic fingerprint from repository identity, exact head, normalized argv or grip id, bounded environment contract, validation profile and resource keys without embedding secret values.
Starting equivalent work while a matching task is active returns the existing task identity and evidence instead of launching a second process; non-equivalent work remains independently claimable.

A completed result is reusable only for the exact head and equivalent inputs within an explicit freshness window; changed source, dependencies, environment, policy or risk profile invalidates reuse and requires fresh validation.

Concurrent validations use per-task temporary homes and build state or an explicit validation lease so one run cannot remove or corrupt another run's fixtures, caches or generated access-policy files.
Cheap deterministic preflight and focused tests run before full validation, while high-risk or merge-bound paths still require the configured complete validation and current-head CI evidence.

Receipts distinguish launched, joined-active, reused-valid, invalidated and forced-fresh decisions and report avoided duplicate work, false-reuse regressions, validation collisions and end-to-end savings.

Tests cover active duplicate joins, changed-head invalidation, environment drift, expired results, secret-free fingerprints, concurrent validation isolation, focused-to-full escalation and required fresh runs for high-risk actions.

metadata.source: Live Grabowski task and friction audit on 2026-07-11
observed_failures: Multiple near-identical full validation tasks were launched for the same repository slice.
Concurrent scheduled validations shared a fixed test-home directory and one run failed after another removed generated files; serialization later passed the full suite.
